report_name: Google Issues Urgent Chrome Update to Patch 60 Flaws, Including Two Critical Bugs

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-5858
vulnerabilities.description: A critical heap buffer overflow vulnerability in Google Chrome's WebML component that could allow for remote code execution if a user is tricked into visiting a malicious website. Affects versions prior to 147.0.7727.55/56.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-5859
vulnerabilities.description: A critical integer overflow vulnerability in Google Chrome's WebML component that could allow for remote code execution if a user is tricked into visiting a malicious website. Affects versions prior to 147.0.7727.55/56.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-5860
vulnerabilities.description: A high-severity use-after-free vulnerability in Google Chrome's WebRTC component. Affects versions prior to 147.0.7727.55/56.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-5861
vulnerabilities.description: A high-severity use-after-free vulnerability in Google Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine. Affects versions prior to 147.0.7727.55/56.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-5862
vulnerabilities.description: A high-severity use-after-free vulnerability in Google Chrome's WebAudio component. Affects versions prior to 147.0.7727.55/56.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-5863
vulnerabilities.description: A high-severity vulnerability due to inappropriate implementation in Google Chrome's Media component. Affects versions prior to 147.0.7727.55/56.

indicators.name: Suspicious Child Process of Google Chrome
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Child Process of Google Chrome
id: 9b8a3c2d-1f4e-4b6a-8d7c-5e9f0a1b2c3d
status: experimental
description: Detects Google Chrome spawning shell processes such as cmd.exe, powershell.exe, or wscript.exe. This could indicate successful remote code execution from a browser exploit.
references:
  - https://www.forbes.com/sites/daveywinder/2026/04/09/google-issues-critical-update-alert-for-35-billion-chrome-users/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-10
modified: 2026-04-10
tags:
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.execution
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate extensions or web applications that interact with the local OS, though this is rare.
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\chrome.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child
indicators.description: Detects when the Google Chrome process spawns a command shell, which could be indicative of code execution resulting from the exploitation of vulnerabilities like CVE-2026-5858 or CVE-2026-5859.

indicators.name: Google Chrome Crash Detected
indicators.pattern:
title: Google Chrome Crash Detected
id: 7e6d5f4c-3b2a-4d1e-9c8b-0a1b2c3d4e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects a crash event related to the Google Chrome process. A spike in these events across an organization could indicate attempts to exploit a memory corruption vulnerability.
references:
  - https://www.forbes.com/sites/daveywinder/2026/04/09/google-issues-critical-update-alert-for-35-billion-chrome-users/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-10
modified: 2026-04-10
tags:
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - Benign browser crashes due to software bugs, extension conflicts, or driver issues.
level: low
logsource:
  product: windows
  service: application
detection:
  selection:
    Provider_Name: 'Windows Error Reporting'
    EventID: 1001
    EventData|contains: 'chrome.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects Windows Error Reporting events for chrome.exe crashes. A sudden increase in these events could indicate attempts to exploit memory corruption vulnerabilities such as CVE-2026-5858.
